name: checkAvailability
description: Check whether a specific asset is free to rent for a date range. Returns available:true/false plus any conflicting bookings, scheduled maintenance windows, or legal/compliance holds that block it. ALWAYS call this before createBooking — never claim an asset is free without it. startDate/endDate are ISO YYYY-MM-DD and are compared against the fixed reference date; startDate must be < endDate.

startDate.description: Rental start date, ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Required.

endDate.description: Rental end date, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

name: listBookings
description: List rental bookings in the current workspace, optionally filtered by status. Each row: id (bk_), assetId, customerId, startDate, endDate, status (pending/confirmed/out/returned/closed/cancelled), and whether a technician is dispatched. Call this to find a real bk_ id — NEVER invent a booking id.

name: getBooking
description: Get full detail for one booking by id: asset, customer, date range, status, dispatched technician, linked quote/invoice, held deposit, and any active hold. Read before checking out, invoicing, or cancelling.

name: createBooking
description: Reserve an asset for a customer over a date range (status becomes "confirmed"). REQUIRES that checkAvailability returned available:true for the same assetId + range — the world re-checks and REJECTS overlaps, assets in maintenance/retired, or assets/accounts under a legal or compliance hold. REJECTED when the workspace is at its active-booking quota (check getPlanUsage). Dates ISO YYYY-MM-DD, must be in the future relative to the reference date, startDate < endDate. Optionally pass quoteId to lock a previously generated price.

customerId.description: Customer the rental is for (from listCustomers). Required.

endDate.description: Rental end, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

quoteId.description: Optional. A qt_ id from generateQuote to lock the quoted price.

name: rescheduleBooking
description: Move a pending/confirmed booking to a new date range. Re-checks availability for the new range and REJECTS conflicts/maintenance/holds. Bookings that are out/returned/closed/cancelled cannot be rescheduled.

endDate.description: New end, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

name: cancelBooking
description: Cancel a booking. DESTRUCTIVE and irreversible — frees the asset and voids any dispatch. Two steps: FIRST call with confirmed=false (or omit it) to get the confirmation prompt, relay it to the user, and STOP. Only AFTER the user explicitly confirms, call again with confirmed=true. A booking already out on rental cannot be cancelled (check it in instead).

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after the user has explicitly confirmed. Defaults to false.

name: checkOutAsset
description: Mark a confirmed booking's asset as picked up / checked out to the customer (status confirmed→out). BLOCKED if the required security deposit is not fully held (call getDepositBalance / chargeDeposit first) or the asset/account is under a hold. Records the checkout condition of the asset.

bookingId.description: The confirmed booking to check out. Required.

conditionOut.description: Optional. Asset condition recorded at checkout (defaults to the asset's current condition).

name: checkInAsset
description: Mark an out booking's asset as returned (status out→returned) and record the return condition. A return condition of "poor"/"damaged" should be followed by fileClaim before releasing the deposit. Returning the asset makes the rental invoiceable (generateInvoice) and the deposit releasable.

bookingId.description: The out booking being returned. Required.

returnedDate.description: Optional. Actual return date, ISO YYYY-MM-DD (defaults to the booking endDate). A date after endDate accrues a late fee at invoice time.

name: closeBooking
description: Finalize a returned booking (status returned→closed). BLOCKED while the invoice is unpaid, the deposit is still held, or an open claim exists against the booking. Read getBooking / getInvoice / getDepositBalance first to see what is outstanding.

bookingId.description: The returned booking to close. Required.

name: listTechnicians
description: List field technicians in the workspace: id (tech_), name, skills, home base, and today's job load. Call this to find a real tech_ id before dispatching — NEVER invent a technician.

skill.description: Optional. Filter to technicians with this skill (e.g. "heavy_equipment", "electrical", "delivery").

name: getTechnicianSchedule
description: Return a technician's scheduled dispatch jobs and free days across a date range, so you can spot conflicts before dispatching. Use these EXACT dates — never claim a technician is free without reading this.

endDate.description: Window end, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

name: dispatchTechnician
description: Assign a technician to a booking's field job (delivery/pickup/onsite_service/inspection) on a date. REJECTED if the technician already has a job on that date (check getTechnicianSchedule first) or the booking is cancelled/closed. Calling again for the same booking reassigns to the new technician/date.

bookingId.description: The booking whose field job is being dispatched. Required.

jobType.description: Optional. Kind of field job (defaults to "delivery").

name: cancelDispatch
description: Remove a technician's field-job assignment from a booking, freeing that technician's day. DESTRUCTIVE. Two steps: FIRST call with confirmed=false (or omit) to get the confirmation prompt, relay it, and STOP; only after the user confirms, call again with confirmed=true.

bookingId.description: The booking whose dispatch to cancel. Required.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: generateQuote
description: Compute a rental quote for an asset over a date range. Returns a NUMERIC breakdown: dailyRate × billableDays + deliveryFee + insuranceFee = total, plus the required securityDeposit. Amounts are computed deterministically from the catalog — quote the EXACT numbers returned, never estimate or round. Produces a qt_ id you can pass to createBooking to lock the price.

endDate.description: Rental end, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

includeDelivery.description: Optional. Add the delivery fee to the quote (defaults to true).

includeInsurance.description: Optional. Add the damage-waiver insurance fee (defaults to false).

name: getQuote
description: Read a rental quote. Pass a quoteId for one quote's full numeric breakdown, or omit it to list all quotes. Report the stored numbers exactly — never re-estimate a total.

quoteId.description: Optional. The quote id (e.g. "qt_5001"). Omit to list all.

name: generateInvoice
description: Generate the rental invoice for a returned booking (status issued). Computes the line items deterministically: rental (dailyRate × billableDays) + deliveryFee + insuranceFee, plus a lateFee line = lateDays × dailyRate × lateMultiplier when the asset was returned after endDate. No money moves here — payInvoice charges it. Idempotent: a booking that already has an invoice returns the existing inv_ id.

bookingId.description: The returned booking to invoice. Required.

name: listInvoices
description: List invoices in the workspace, optionally filtered by status. Each row: id (inv_), bookingId, total, balanceDue, status (draft/issued/partially_paid/paid/void/overdue). Call this to find a real inv_ id — NEVER invent an invoice id or amount.

name: getInvoice
description: Get full detail for one invoice: line items, subtotal, fees, lateFee, total, amountPaid, balanceDue, and status. Ground any amount you state in these numbers — never fabricate a total.

name: getDepositBalance
description: Read security-deposit balances. Pass a bookingId for that booking's required vs held deposit and any shortfall; omit it for the workspace deposit-float summary (total held, total required). Never claim a deposit is covered or refundable without reading this.

bookingId.description: Optional. A booking to read the deposit for. Omit for the workspace float summary.

name: chargeDeposit
description: Charge / place a security-deposit hold for a booking. MOVES money. Two steps: FIRST call with confirmed=false (or omit) to return the amount + confirmation prompt and STOP; only after the user confirms, call again with confirmed=true. Amount defaults to the asset's required deposit; pass amount for a partial top-up. Idempotent — a booking already fully held is reported, not double-charged.

bookingId.description: The booking to place the deposit for. Required.

amount.description: Optional. Amount to hold (defaults to the asset's required deposit).

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after the user has explicitly confirmed the charge. Defaults to false.

name: releaseDeposit
description: Release a held security deposit back to the customer. MOVES money and cannot be undone. BLOCKED while an open damage claim or a legal/compliance hold exists against the booking, asset, or customer account. Two-step confirm (confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true). Pass amount for a partial release, capped at the amount held.

bookingId.description: The booking whose deposit to release. Required.

amount.description: Optional. Partial amount to release (capped at the held amount).

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: payInvoice
description: Record a payment against an invoice. MOVES money. Two-step confirm: FIRST confirmed=false (or omit) to return the amount + prompt and STOP; only after the user confirms, confirmed=true. Idempotent via an optional idempotencyKey and an already-paid guard — never double-charge. Pass amount for a partial payment, capped at balanceDue.

amount.description: Optional. Amount to pay (defaults to the full balanceDue; capped at it).

idempotencyKey.description: Optional. A caller-supplied key; a repeat call with the same key is a no-op returning the first result.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after the user has explicitly confirmed the charge. Defaults to false.

name: issueRefund
description: Refund money against a paid invoice. CAPPED at amountPaid — partial allowed, over-cap REJECTED. MOVES money and cannot be undone. BLOCKED while a compliance/legal hold freezes the account. Two-step confirm (confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true).

invoiceId.description: The paid invoice to refund. Required.

amount.description: Amount to refund, capped at amountPaid. Required.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: voidInvoice
description: Void an issued/overdue invoice (status→void). DESTRUCTIVE. A PAID invoice cannot be voided — issue a refund instead. Two steps: confirmed=false first → confirmation prompt → confirmed=true only after the user agrees.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: listClaims
description: List damage/incident claims in the workspace, optionally filtered by status. Each row: id (clm_), type, status (submitted/under_review/approved/denied/settled), linked bookingId/assetId. Call this to find a real clm_ id — NEVER invent a claim.

name: getClaim
description: Get full detail for one claim: type, status, description, evidence labels, linked booking/asset/customer, and settlementAmount. Read this before resolving a claim or discussing a deposit while a claim is open.

name: fileClaim
description: File a damage/incident claim (types: damage, loss, injury, late_return) against a booking OR an asset — pass at least one of bookingId/assetId. Provide a description and evidence references (attachment labels the user uploaded). Filing AUTOMATICALLY places an investigatory hold that freezes the asset until the claim is resolved — do NOT promise the deposit back while a claim is open.

bookingId.description: The booking the claim is against (pass this or assetId).

assetId.description: The asset the claim is against (pass this or bookingId).

evidence.description: Optional. Attachment labels supporting the claim.

name: addClaimEvidence
description: Attach more evidence labels to a claim that is still submitted/under_review. Rejected once the claim is resolved (approved/denied/settled).

evidence.description: Attachment labels to add. Required, non-empty.

name: resolveClaim
description: Resolve a claim: "approve" (deduct settlementAmount from the booking's security deposit), "deny" (no charge), or "settle" (record an agreed settlementAmount). Approving/settling MOVES money — two-step confirm (confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true). Resolving lifts the claim's investigatory hold.

settlementAmount.description: Amount to charge against the deposit for approve/settle. Required for approve/settle.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation (for approve/settle). Defaults to false.

name: listHolds
description: List active legal/compliance/safety/payment holds in the workspace. Each hold: id (hold_), type, scope (asset/account/workspace), the frozen assetId/customerId, and reason. OTHER operations (createBooking, checkOutAsset, releaseDeposit, issueRefund, retireAsset, transferAsset) are gated on these — ALWAYS read holds before promising an asset or moving money.

assetId.description: Optional. Only holds affecting this asset.

name: placeHold
description: Place a legal/compliance/safety/payment hold that FREEZES an asset, a customer account, or the whole workspace. While active it blocks new bookings, checkouts, deposit releases, and refunds on the frozen entity. Additive and protective — no confirm needed. Pass assetId for scope=asset, customerId for scope=account.

reason.description: Why the hold is placed. Required, non-empty.

customerId.description: The frozen customer account (required when scope=account).

name: releaseHold
description: Lift a legal/compliance/safety/payment hold, un-freezing the entity. DESTRUCTIVE — removing a compliance/legal freeze is high-risk. Verify the reason (and lookupPolicy "hold_release") before releasing. Two steps: confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true only after explicit user confirmation.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: listCustomers
description: List customers in the workspace: id (cust_), name, and whether the account has an active hold or outstanding balance. Call this to find a real cust_ id before booking or reading a record — NEVER invent a customer.

name: getCustomer
description: Read a customer record: id (cust_), name, masked contact, rental-history summary, outstanding balance, and any account-level holds. PII-SENSITIVE — share only what the user needs and never fabricate customer details.

name: createCustomer
description: Register a new customer record and return its cust_ id. Requires a name and a contact email. PII-sensitive. Use before booking for a customer who is not already in listCustomers.

name: lookupPolicy
description: Look up an operating/compliance policy by topic. Ground any policy claim (deposit rules, damage liability, when a hold may be released, cancellation windows) in the returned text — NEVER invent a rule or a number.

name: listAssets
description: List the asset registry / rental catalog, optionally filtered by category or status. Each row: id (ast_, e.g. "ast_excv01"), name, category, condition, status (available/reserved/out/maintenance/retired), dailyRate, requiredDeposit. Call this to find a real asset id and its rate — NEVER invent an asset or a price.

name: getAsset
description: Full asset detail: name, category, condition, current status, dailyRate, requiredDeposit, availability windows, open maintenance, and any active hold. Read this before quoting, booking, or checking out — never state a rate or availability you have not read.

name: registerAsset
description: Add a new asset to the fleet registry and return its ast_ id (minted from the category, e.g. ast_excv02). Requires a name, category, dailyRate, and requiredDeposit; a new asset starts status=available.

name.description: Human name/model of the asset. Required.

requiredDeposit.description: Security deposit required to rent it. Required.

name: updateAssetCondition
description: Update an asset's recorded condition (excellent…damaged), e.g. after an inspection. This is an out-of-band correction; checkInAsset already records the return condition. Setting condition to "damaged" does NOT auto-file a claim — call fileClaim separately.

name: scheduleMaintenance
description: Put an asset into maintenance for a date window, making it unavailable to book in that window (status→maintenance). REJECTED if the asset is currently out on an active rental. Returns a maintenance record.

startDate.description: Maintenance window start, ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Required.

endDate.description: Maintenance window end, ISO YYYY-MM-DD, after startDate. Required.

name: completeMaintenance
description: Return an asset from maintenance to service (status maintenance→available) and record the resulting condition. Only assets currently in maintenance can be completed.

name: getMaintenanceLog
description: Read the maintenance history for an asset (past and scheduled windows, reasons, resulting conditions). Never invent a maintenance record.

name: retireAsset
description: Permanently retire an asset from the fleet (status→retired). DESTRUCTIVE and irreversible. BLOCKED if the asset is out on rental, reserved by a future booking, or under a hold. Two steps: confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true only after explicit user confirmation.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: transferAsset
description: Transfer an asset to another workspace/location (leaves this workspace's fleet). DESTRUCTIVE from this workspace's view. BLOCKED while the asset is out on rental, reserved by a future booking, or under a hold. Two steps: confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true only after explicit user confirmation.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: getWorkspace
description: Read the current workspace/tenant: id (ws_), name, plan tier, status (active/suspended), and whether onboarding is complete. All operations act within THIS workspace only — never mix tenants or reference another workspace's data.

name: getPlanUsage
description: Read plan quotas vs current usage: seat cap & seats used, active-booking cap & bookings used this cycle, and the deposit-float limit. OTHER operations (createBooking, inviteMember) are gated on these — read before promising a booking or a seat. Returns atSeatCap / atBookingCap booleans.

name: listMembers
description: List workspace members: id (mem_), name, role (owner/admin/dispatcher/billing/viewer), and status. Call this to find a real mem_ id — NEVER invent a member.

name: getMember
description: Read one member's role and exact permission set (canManageMembers, canMoveMoney, canDispatch, canManageFleet). Pass memberId for a specific member, or omit it for the acting user. Check permissions here before attempting a privileged op.

memberId.description: Optional. The member to read; omit for the acting user.

name: inviteMember
description: Invite a new member with a role. CONSUMES a seat — REJECTED when the workspace is at its seat cap (check getPlanUsage). PRIVILEGED: requires the acting user to have canManageMembers (owner/admin).

role.description: Role to grant. Required (a new invite cannot be owner).

name: updateMemberRole
description: Change a member's role. PRIVILEGED — requires the acting user to be owner/admin (canManageMembers). Cannot demote the sole remaining owner.

name: removeMember
description: Remove a member from the workspace, freeing their seat. DESTRUCTIVE and irreversible. PRIVILEGED (owner/admin). Cannot remove the sole owner. Two steps: confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true only after explicit user confirmation.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: changePlan
description: Change the workspace plan tier (starter/pro/fleet/enterprise). Adjusts seat and booking caps and MAY change billing. PRIVILEGED (owner). Downgrading below current usage (seats or active bookings over the new cap) is REJECTED. Two steps: confirmed=false first → prompt → confirmed=true only after explicit user confirmation.

confirmed.description: Set true ONLY after explicit user confirmation. Defaults to false.

name: getAuditLog
description: Read the workspace activity log (who did what: recent privileged actions, money movements, holds). Use to answer "did X happen / when" — NEVER fabricate history. Optionally filter by action or limit the number of entries.

action.description: Optional. Filter to a specific action name (e.g. "payInvoice", "placeHold").

limit.description: Optional. Max entries to return (defaults to 20).
